title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gas in stomach
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for gas in stomach are natural extracts derived from plants that help alleviate digestive discomfort by reducing bloating, cramping, and flatulence. They work by stimulating digestion, relaxing muscles, and reducing inflammation in the stomach. The best herbal essential oils for gas in stomach include Foeniculum vulgare, Zingiber officinale, Cuminum cyminum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Cinnamomum verum, Coriandrum sativum, Syzygium aromaticum, Anethum graveolens, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, gas in the stomach can lead to discomfort, indigestion, and embarrassment, affecting daily activities, social interactions, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with gas in stomach because of its natural calming properties that relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping. The menthol content also helps to thin mucus in the stomach, making it easier to pass gas and relieve bloating. Additionally, peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by gas buildup. Overall, inhaling or applying peppermint essential oil topically can provide quick relief from symptoms of indigestion and discomfort associated with gas in the stomach.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Peppermint essential oil has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to calm spasms and ease symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort caused by gas.

Carminative Properties: The menthol content in peppermint essential oil helps to release trapped gas bubbles from the digestive system, allowing them to be passed more easily and relieving pressure and discomfort.
Antispasmodic Effect: Peppermint essential oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to gas and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from gas and bloating.
Stems: The stems contain a higher concentration of menthone, which is effective in relaxing the muscles in the digestive system, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause gas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the morning when they are most fragrant.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves with clean water and mild soap to remove dirt and debris then rinse thoroughly.
Place 500g of washed peppermint leaves into a stainless steel distillation apparatus for 6 hours and collect steam.
Cool the distilled essential oil by placing it in an ice bath or refrigerating for at least 30 minutes before use.
Transfer the cooled mentha x piperita essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain.

Calming and soothing property: Chamomile oil's calming and soothing effects on the nervous system can help to ease anxiety and stress-related symptoms that can exacerbate digestive issues like gas and bloating in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce stomach discomfort.
Flowers: High in azulene, a potent antioxidant that helps calm inflammation and reduce spasms in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and wash them gently with clean water.
Combine 250g of the cleaned flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing it to steep.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids.
Transfer the filtered liquid to a clean glass bottle and seal it tightly storing it at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with gas in stomach because of its natural digestive properties. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to increase digestion and absorption of nutrients, reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce inflammation and soothe any irritation that may be contributing to gas and indigestion. By using herbal ginger essential oils, individuals can promote healthy digestion and alleviate uncomfortable symptoms associated with gas in the stomach.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger essential oil stimulates digestive enzymes, which helps to break down food more efficiently and reduce symptoms of bloating and gas.
Spasmolytic: The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger essential oil help to relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to gas and discomfort.

Antioxidant: Ginger essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress in the digestive system, leading to inflammation and further exacerbating symptoms of bloating and gas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the thick, underground stems of the ginger plant that have a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them ideal for extracting essential oils that can help alleviate stomach gas and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger rhizomes free of pests diseases and molds weighing about 100 grams.
Dry the ginger rhizomes by spreading them thinly on paper towels for at least 7 days or until moisture content is 10.
Steam distill the dried ginger using a steam distillation apparatus with a condenser for 4-5 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the finished zingiber officinale essential oil in an amber glass bottle away from direct sunlight at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from your garden or store-bought dried ones for at least 100 grams quantity.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm and well-ventilated area for approximately 48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers using a copper still with 1 liter of distilled water for about 4 hours to extract essential oils.
Collect and filter the resulting oil from the distillation process through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into glass bottles.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to one year, using airtight lids and labels.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare seeds and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks in a warm dark place.
Combine 200 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds with 400 milliliters of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for seven days at room temperature away from direct sunlight allowing it to macerate and release its oils.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Allow the infused liquid to mature in a cool dark place for four weeks before distilling it using a low-pressure steam distillation apparatus to produce the essential oil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest anethum graveolens leaves at dawn when they are most potent for digestive issues.

Steep the mixture in a glass container at room temperature for 14 days to allow for optimal extraction.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store the essential oil in dark glass bottles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi seeds measure out one hundred grams of the dried seeds.

Crush the dried carum carvi seeds into fine powder using a mortar and pestle take about thirty minutes.
Combine the crushed seed powder with four ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil stir well to blend.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for forty-eight hours shaking occasionally before straining through cheesecloth to separate oils.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from the plant to use for distillation.
